description: Input validation audit and hardening: Add comprehensive input validation to critical endpoints that lack it, protecting against malformed data that could corrupt the journal or crash modules during event night.
steps: In server/modules/people.mod.js (or equivalent endpoint handling person creation/updates): Add validation for name (max 100 chars, non-empty), roles (must be valid ROLES array values), code (max 20 chars, alphanumeric+dash pattern), ort (max 100 chars), notizen (max 2000 chars). Any field exceeding limits should be truncated or rejected with 400.

In server/modules/chat.mod.js: Add validation for message text (max 2000 chars, must be non-empty after trim). Channel names (max 50 chars). This prevents someone pasting enormous text blobs that bloat the journal.
In server/modules/announce.mod.js: Add validation for announcement text (max 1000 chars), level must be one of the valid levels, scope must be a valid object structure.
In server/modules/tasks.mod.js: Add validation for task title (max 200 chars, required), note (max 2000 chars), deadline format validation (HH:MM pattern), prio must be valid value.
In server/modules/breaks.mod.js: Add validation for break request note (max 500 chars).

In server/kernel/http.js parseBody(): Add a JSON depth/size sanity check - if the parsed JSON has more than 50 nested levels or the body exceeds the route's size limit, reject with 400. This prevents deeply-nested JSON bombs.
Add test cases to smoke.mjs: verify that oversized text fields are properly truncated or rejected, verify deeply nested JSON is rejected.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes including new input validation tests
Posting a chat message > 2000 chars gets truncated or returns 400
Creating a task with a title > 200 chars gets truncated or returns 400
verification: Run npm test and confirm all checks pass
findings: All modules now have comprehensive input validation. The project prefers truncation over rejection for text content (confirmed by existing patterns in incidents.mod.js and announce.mod.js). Key changes: (1) chat text limit raised to 2000 (from 1000) matching the acceptance criteria; (2) announce text limit raised to 1000 (from 500) for longer messages; (3) tasks title limit changed from 160 to 200 per spec; (4) JSON depth check uses a lightweight character-scan approach that correctly skips string literals containing braces; (5) people.mod.js now imports ROLES from auth.js for validation; (6) The actor token in smoke.mjs had an existing break from demo seed data, so the break note test uses the catering token instead; (7) breaks.mod.js durationMin is now clamped between 1-120 to prevent abuse. Total tests: 164 (12 new).
